title: KOLASIB DC HOVIN DISTRICT CONSULTATIVE COMMITTEE (DCC) THUKHAWM, BANK HNATHAWH DAN THLIRHO A NI
content:
699/2026-2027

Kolasib, 25th May, 2026: Vawiin khan DC Conference Hall, Kolasib-ah Kolasib District Consultative Committee (DCC) te chu an thukhawm a, Kolasib District chhunga bank hrang hrangte hnathawh dan leh hmabak awmte an thlirho. He thutkhawm hi Kolasib Deputy Commissioner leh DCC Chairman ni bawk, Pu H. Dolianbuaia'n a kaihruai a ni.

Thutkhawm kaihruaitu DC Pu H. Dolianbuaia chuan thu sawiin, mipuite leh khawtlang hmasawnna atan bank te chu innghahna pawimawh tak an nih thu a tarlang a. Mipuiten sawrkar scheme hrang hrang an chhawr tangkai theih nan leh eizawnna kawnga hma an sawn theih nan bank ten mawhphurhna lian tak an neih thu a sawi. DC chuan, "Mipui harsatnate hriatthiam puia, dawhthei tak leh tha taka lo dawngsawng thin turin bank hnathawkte ka ngen a che u," tiin, department atanga scheme thehluhte pawh a hun taka lo tifel a, a theih chin chinah mipuite tana awlsam zawka thil kalpui thin a tul thu a sawi bawk.

He hunah hian Pu Thuamzamung Tunglut, Lead District Manager (LDM) chuan kum 2025-26 chhunga Kolasib District-a bank branch hrang hrangte hmalakna leh an dinhmun tlangpui report a pe a. Report-a a lan danin, March 2026 thlenga Kolasib District chhunga bank hrang hrangte Credit-Deposit (CD) Ratio khaikhawm chu 63.36% a ni a, hei hi kum hmasa dinhmun aiin 0.31% in a sang zawk a ni.

Meeting-ah hian District Credit Plan kalpui mek dan te, mipuite tana hmalakna hrang hrang leh Central Scheme kalpui dan te chipchiar taka sawiho a ni a. Heng hmalakna kawnga ti tha tawk lo bank awmte chu an tlin lohna chhan leh harsatnate zawh fiah nghal an ni. Hei bakah hian kum 2026-27 atana District Annual Credit Plan kalpui turte pawh thlirho nghal a ni bawk.

Vawiin thutkhawmah hian Reserve Bank of India (RBI) atangin Pu Harish Vedi, Lead District Officer (LDO) a telpui a. Kolasib District chhunga bank branch hrang hrang hotute, Agriculture, AH & Vety, Horticulture leh Industries department aiawh hotute bakah NABARD hotute an tel a ni.

Attachment

title: Union Minister Shri Rajiv Ranjan Singh Announces Various Initiatives to Strengthen Mizoram’s Dairy Sector During Visit to MULCO Dairy
content:
108/2026-2027

Aizawl, 25 May 2026:Union Minister for Fisheries, Animal Husbandry & Dairying and Panchayati Raj, Rajiv Ranjan Singh, visited the MULCO Dairy at Thuampui today and announced several initiatives aimed at strengthening the cooperative dairy sector in Mizoram.

The programme was attended by Union Minister of State for Fisheries, Animal Husbandry & Dairying and Minority Affairs George Kurian, Union Minister of State for Fisheries, Animal Husbandry & Dairying and Panchayati Raj S. P. Singh Baghel, Mizoram’s Animal Husbandry & Veterinary Minister C. Lalsawivunga, Cooperation Minister P. C. Vanlalruata, Fisheries Minister Lalthansanga, along with senior government officials, representatives from the National Dairy Development Board (NDDB), officials from Mizoram Milk Producers' Cooperative Union Limited, cooperative society members, and farmers.

At the MULCO Dairy premises, the Union Minister and his delegation were welcomed by MULCO management and officials. The occasion also included the inauguration of the Mizo Café and the FPO Office-cum-Sales Outlet.

Following this, the Union Minister visited the MULCO Dairy Processing Plant, where NDDB officials briefed him on plans to upgrade and strengthen the 20 TLPD milk processing plant and presented their requirements.

In his speech, Minister Shri Rajiv Ranjan Singh expressed appreciation for the progress made by MULCO and stated that the advancement achieved through the efforts of dairy cooperatives and workers was commendable. He assured continued cooperation between the Central and State Governments to sustain this development.

The Minister noted that milk production in Mizoram remains low and is insufficient to meet local demand. He stressed that Mizoram should not lag behind neighboring northeastern states and urged farmers and officials to work with renewed commitment to improve the sector.

Announcing several initiatives, the Minister described as encouraging news for farmers and the state government the revision of the Central Government’s funding pattern. The earlier 75:25 sharing ratio has now been changed to 90:10, significantly reducing the state government’s financial burden and helping accelerate developmental activities.

He further stated that milk processing plants and village-level procurement systems would continue to be upgraded. In collaboration with NDDB, quality cattle breeds through Artificial Insemination and improved cattle feed (fodder) would be made more accessible to farmers. MULCO products manufactured within Mizoram — including paneer, rasmalai, and ghee — would also be marketed more extensively in Aizawl and nearby markets.

The Minister emphasized that the Central Government is committed to providing maximum support for the development of the North East, but the real success of these initiatives depends on the dedication of the State Government and livestock farmers.

During the programme, several new initiatives and products were launched, including MULCO Cow Ghee and various products such as baled silage, Type-II cattle feed, dewormers, and mineral mixture products.

Certificates related to fisheries were also distributed during the event. Fisheries KCC cards, Best Startup in Fisheries awards, and Best Fisheries Cooperative recognitions were presented as well.

Commissioner & Secretary of the Cooperation Department, Udit Prakash Rai, explained various plans and initiatives for strengthening Mizoram’s cooperative dairy sector. NDDB Chairman Meenesh Shah highlighted the importance of strengthening dairy cooperatives and improving milk-processing infrastructure in Mizoram. Farmers under MULCO and fish farmers also shared how they benefited from Central Government initiatives and spoke about their work.

The programme concluded with a vote of thanks delivered by Animal Husbandry & Veterinary Department Secretary and MULCO Chairman Lalsangliana.

Attachment

title: Union Minister of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying Chairs Regional Review Meeting of Northeastern States
content:
107/2026-2027

Dated Aizawl, the 25th May, 2026:Union Minister of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying, Shri Rajiv Ranjan Singh ji, alias Lalan Singh, who is currently on an official visit to Mizoram, today chaired a Regional Review Meeting of the eight Northeastern states — Arunachal Pradesh, Assam, Manipur, Meghalaya, Mizoram, Nagaland, Sikkim and Tripura — at the High Court Hall, MINECO, to review the progress of the Northeastern States and the implementation of various Central schemes in the region.

The meeting was attended by Minister of State of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying(FAH&D) Shri George Kurian ji, Ministers in charge of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying from the Northeastern states, Joint Secretary of FAH&D Shri Sagar Mehra ji, Chairman of the National Dairy Development Board(NDDB) Shri Meenesh Shah ji, members from National Fisheries Development Board (NFDB) along with officials from the Central and State Governments.

Expressing gratitude to the Government of Mizoram for their hospitality and organizing today's event, Minister Shri Rajiv Ranjan Singh stated that the Central Government aims to showcase the beauty and diversity of the Northeastern states and that he was delighted to witness the hidden beauty of Mizoram. Highlighting Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi’s vision for the development of the Northeast, the Minister said that the Central Government is committed to doing everything possible to realize that vision. He emphasized that development in fisheries, animal husbandry and dairy sectors could significantly contribute to the economic development of the Northeast and added that these sectors would bring progress not only for individuals but for the entire region.

Speaking specifically on fisheries, the Minister highlighted various initiatives launched under the leadership of Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi, including the Blue Revolution, the Fisheries and Aquaculture Infrastructure Development Fund (FIDF), and Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PM-MSY). The Minister stated that owing to these initiatives, fish production in the Northeastern region increased from 3.78 lakh tonnes in 2014 to 7.05 lakh tonnes in 2025. He further stated that over the past 12 years (2014–2025), the Central Government had provided a total of Rs. 2,228 crore to the Northeastern states, distributed as follows:

Assam – Rs. 614.11 crore

Arunachal Pradesh – Rs. 246.03 crore

Manipur – Rs. 236.41 crore

Meghalaya – Rs. 240.32 crore

Mizoram – Rs. 246.79 crore

Nagaland – Rs. 209.49 crore

Sikkim – Rs. 109.41 crore

Tripura – Rs. 322.23 crore

The Minister expressed his delight at attending the inauguration of India's first Integrated Aquapark at Ziro in Arunachal Pradesh and announced that out of 11 more planned Integrated Aquaparks, 4 would be established in the Northeast region. He invited Mizoram to submit a Detailed Project Report (DPR) and complete the necessary land acquisition formalities to set up a similar facility in the State. Shri Rajiv Ranjan Singh ji also announced that the central government has approved the Mizoram government’s proposal to establish a Fish Marketing Center at an estimated cost of ₹50 crore in the State.

Additionally, Shri Rajiv Ranjan Singh ji highlighted the immense potential for ornamental fisheries, coldwater fisheries, and inland fisheries in the Northeast. He noted that the central government wishes to promote cluster-based fish farming by setting up 8 notified clusters in the region, upgrade the existing National Fisheries Development Board facility in Assam, and digitally onboard all fish farmers in the area. The Minister further stated that the government is ready to invest ₹200–300 crore under the Mission Amrit Sarovar in the Northeast. He promised that after the monsoon season, top officials from the NDDB, NFDB, and the central ministry would tour the northeastern states to assess their needs and potential first-hand.

On animal husbandry, the Minister stated that the Northeastern states had achieved growth of 37% in milk production, 40% in egg production and 15% in meat production. However, he urged the states to use this progress as fresh momentum to unleash their true potential. The Minister also stressed the importance of strengthening cooperative societies across the Northeast, expanding artificial insemination services, improving infrastructure, promoting technology adoption and enhancing human resource capacity building. Referring to livestock diseases, the Minister said the Central Government is making every effort to develop vaccines for disease prevention. He also announced plans to establish an NDDB Regional Office in Guwahati.

Sharing his vision for the northeastern states to achieve self-sufficiency in fisheries, animal husbandry, and dairy production, the Union Minister invited State ministers and officials to work hand-in-hand with the Center, monitor the implementation of various schemes, adopt best practices, and ensure timely completion of projects.

Minister of State Shri George Kurian ji also spoke on the occasion, stating that the Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying is working towards improving livelihoods, empowering women and promoting entrepreneurship among the people of the Northeast. He urged the northeastern states to become self-reliant, move away from depending on outside supplies, and actively promote their own state-produced goods.

Secretary of the Department of Animal Husbandry and Dairying Shri Naresh Pal Gangwar also addressed the gathering and stated that the Central Government is fully committed to supporting the self-reliance of Northeastern states. He urged all states to work diligently and understand that the success of various government schemes depends largely on their active participation.

Also speaking at the event, Secretary of the Department of Fisheries Shri Abhilaksh Likhi said that efforts are underway to develop the Northeastern states into export hubs. He also encouraged the states to freely communicate their needs to the Central Government.

During the meeting, Ministers from the Northeastern states in charge of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying presented the status, achievements and future plans of their respective states and also highlighted common challenges faced in the region. They emphasized the importance of youth training, cold chain storage facilities, greater digital deployment, infrastructure development and timely release of funds for the region.

At today's meeting, Shri Sudhir Kumar, Principal Secretary , GAD, Mizoram and Chief Secretary(i/c) welcomed the gathering. Joint Secretary of Fisheries, Shri Sagar Mehra, presented the 'NER Vision 2030' and explained the various schemes and programs under the Dept. of Fisheries via a PowerPoint presentation. Chairman of the NDDB, Shri Meenesh Shah, briefed the gathering on future dairy development prospects in the North East Region, while Additional Secretary of the Dept. of Animal Husbandry and Dairying, Smt. Varsha Joshi, spoke on current initiatives and upcoming plans in the livestock sector.

During the meeting, the Union Minister also officially launched a baseline village-level survey to expand the scope of Multipurpose Dairy Cooperative Societies (MDCS), a film on African Swine Fever, an educational booklet on 'Package of practices on Pig Farming', and various projects and schemes tailored for the northeastern states. The event concluded with a vote of thanks delivered by Shri Bijay Kumar Behera, Chief Executive of the National Fisheries Development Board (NFDB).

Attachment

title: Union Minister Rajiv Ranjan Singh-in MULCO Dairy tlawhin,Mizoram Dairy Sector tihchakna tur hmalakna hrang hrang a tlangzarh
content:
698/2026-2027

Aizawl, 25 May 2026 :Fisheries, Animal Husbandry & Dairying leh Panchayati Raj Union Minister Pu Rajiv Ranjan Singh (alias Lalan Singh) chuan vawiin khan Thuampui-a MULCO Dairy a tlawh a, Mizorama cooperative dairy sector tihchakna tur hmalakna hrang hrang a tlangzarh.

He programme-ah hian Union Minister of State for Fisheries, Animal Husbandry & Dairying leh Minority Affairs changtu Pu George Kurian, Union Minister of State for Fisheries, Animal Husbandry & Dairying leh Panchayati Raj changtu Prof S.P Singh Baghel,Mizoram sawrkar hnuaia Animal Husbandry Veterinary Minister Pu C.Lasawivunga, Cooperation Minister Pu P.C. Vanlalruata, Fisheries Department changtu Minister Pu Lalthansanga te bakah sorkar mi pawimawhte, National Dairy Development Board (NDDB) aiawhte, Mizoram Milk Union Limited (MULCO), State sawrkar AH&Vety, Fisheries leh Cooperation Department hotute, cooperative society member-te leh farmer-te an tel a ni.

MULCO Dairy hmunah hian Union Minister leh amah tawiawmtute chu Mizoram Milk Union Manager leh official-ten an lo dawngsawng a, Mizo Café leh FPO Office-cum-Sale Outlet hawnna hun tawi hman nghal a ni.

Hemi zawh hian Union Minister chuan MULCO Dairy Processing Plant a tlawh zui nghal a; National Dairy Development Board (NDDB) official-ten milk processing plant 20 TLPD tihchangtlun leh tihchak dan tur sawipuiin, an mamawhte an thlen a ni.

Minister Pu Rajiv Ranjan Singh chuan thusawiin Mizoram Milk Producers' Co-operative Union Ltd (MULCO) hmasawnna hmel hmuh tur awm mek chungchangah lawmthu a sawi a. Dairy cooperative-te leh thawktute hmalakna avanga MULCO-in hma a sawn hret hret chu lawmawm a tih thu tarlangin, he hmasawnna hi chhunzawm zel a nih theih nan Union leh State Sorkar te chu thawk hovin hma an lak chhunzawm zel tur thu a tiam a ni.

Minister chuan Mizoram chhunga bawnghnute thar chhuah (milk production) dinhmun chu a la tlem hle tih a tarlang a. Hei hian kan mamawh min la phuhruk tawk lo a ni tih a sawi. Kan chhehvel state-te lakah kan hnufual bik tur a ni lo, tiin, NE state dangte kan umphak a, kan pelh theih nan ranvulhtu leh official-te chu tan la thar zel turin a ngen a ni.

Minister chuan hmalakna tur hrang hrang sawiin, ranvulhtute leh state sawrkar tana chanchin lawmawm tak chu, Central Sawrkar-in sum (matching share) a thawh dan a thlak danglam hi a ni a,75:25 ni thin chu tunah chuan 90:10-ah thlak a ni tawh dawn a ni. Hei hian state sawrkar sum thawh tur nasa taka tih-hniam avangin hmasawnna hnathawhte a chak phah hle dawn a ni, a ti.

Milk processing plant-te leh village-level procurement system-te tihchangtlun zel a ni anga, NDDB nen thawk hovin bawng chi tha (Artificial Insemination) leh ran chaw (fodder) tha farmers-te hnenah awlsam zawka chhawp chhuah a ni ang.MULCO hmangin Mizoram chhunga thar chhuah ngei—paneer, rasmalai, leh bawnghnute hriak (ghee) te chu Aizawl leh a chhehvel bazaar-ah nasa zawka zawrh chhuah a ni ang.

Central Sawrkar chuan North East bial hmasawnna turin thlawpna tha ber pek a inhuam a, amaherawhchu heng hmalakna hlawhtlinna tak tak chu State Sawrkar leh ranvulhtute tan laknaah a innghat a ni, a ti.

Programme neih chhung hian hmalakna thar leh product hrang hrang tlangzarh niin, chung zingah chuan MULCO Cow Ghee bakah khawl chi hrang hrang - baled silage, Type-II cattle feed, dewormer leh mineral mixture product-te pawh tlangzarh an ni.

He programme-ah hian Fisheries kaihhnawih certificate-te pawh sem chhuah a ni a. Fisheries KCC card te, Best Startup in Fisheries award leh Best Fisheries Cooperative recognition-te pawh hlan an ni bawk.

Cooperation Department Commissioner & Secretary Pu Udit Prakash Rai chuan Mizorama cooperative dairy sector tihchakna tur hmalakna leh ruahman hrang hrang a sawifiah a.NDDB Chairman Dr. Meenesh Shah chuan Mizorama dairy cooperative tihchak leh milk processing infrastructure tihhmasawn a pawimawhzia a sawi a. MULCO hnuaia farmer te leh fish farmer ten sawrkar laipui hmalakna atanga hmasawnna an hmuh te leh an hnathawh dan sawina hun an hmang bawk.

Animal Husbandry & Veterinary Department Secretary leh MULCO Chairman Pu Lalsangliana’n lawmthu sawiin hun a khar a ni.

title: Union Minister of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying hovin Hmarchhak State-ten Regional Review Meeting an nei
content:
697/2026-2027

Dated Aizawl, the 25th May, 2026 :Vawiin khan Mizoram tlawha lo zin mek Union Minister of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying Pu Rajiv Ranjan Singh alias Lalan Singh kaihhruaiin hmarchhak state pariat(8) - Arunachal Pradesh, Assam, Manipur, Meghalaya, Mizoram, Nagaland, Sikkim leh Tripura-te chuan High Court Hall, MINECO-ah hmarchhak state-te hmalakna leh sawrkar laipui hnuaia scheme hrang hrang hman tangkai dante thlir hona (Regional Review Meeting) an nei a. He hunah hian Minister of State of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying(FAH&D)Pu George Kurian, hmarchhak state tin atangin Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying changtu Minister-te, FAH&D Joint Secretary Pu Sagar Mehra, National Dairy Development Board(NDDB) Chairman Pu Meenesh Shah te bakah Central leh State official dangte an tel bawk a ni.

Mizoram sawrkar hnenah vawiin hun an lo buatsaih leh an lo dawnsawn avangin lawmthu sawiin, Minister Pu Rajiv Ranjan Singh chuan sawrkar laipui chuan hmarchhak state-te mawina leh nihphung hrang hrangte phochhuah a duh thu sawiin Mizoram mawina inthup mawi tak mai a hmu chhuak chu nuam a tih thu a sawi bawk. Minister chuan PM Modi-a’n hmarchhak bial hmasawnna tura vision a neih chu tarlangin, Central Sawrkar chuan chu vision puitlin a nih theih nan a theih apiang tih a duh thu a sawi a. Sangha khawi, Ran vulh leh Bawnghnute thar lama hmasawnna hian hmarchhak bialah economic development tha tak a thlen theih thu sawiin, Minister chuan he sector hian mimal hmasawnna chauh ni lovin, bial pum hmasawnna a thlen dawn a ni, a ti.

Sangha khawi bik chungchangah thu sawiin, Minister chuan PM Modi-a kaihhruaina hnuaiah Blue Revolution, Fisheries and Aquaculture Infrastructure Development Fund (FIDF), PM-Matsya Sampada Yojana(PM-MSY) leh hmalakna dang tam tak a awm thu a tarlang a. Heng hmalaknate avangin hmarchhak biala sangha thar chhuah chu kum 2014-a tonne nuai 3.78 thin kha kum 2025-ah chuan tonne nuai 7.05-ah a kaichhoh thu a sawi a ni. Hemi bakah hian, Minister chuan kum 12 (2014-2025) kal ta chhung khan sawrkar laipui chuan cheng vaibelchhe 2,228 zet hmarchhak state-te a lo pek tawh thu a sawi a. Chu chu hetiang hian then a ni:

Assam - cheng vaibelchhe 614.11

Arunachal Pradesh - cheng vaibelchhe 246.03

Manipur - cheng vaibelchhe 236.41

Meghalaya - cheng vaibelchhe 240.32

Mizoram - cheng vaibelchhe 246.79

Nagaland - cheng vaibelchhe 209.49

Sikkim - cheng vaibelchhe 109.41

Tripura - cheng vaibelchhe 322.23

Minister chuan India rama Integrated Aquapark hmasa ber Ziro, Arunachal Pradesh-a hawnnaa a tel thei chu lawmawm a tih thu sawiin, Integrated Aquapark dang 11 din tura ruahman zinga 4 chu hmarchhak biala din a nih dawn thu a sawi a. Minister chuan Mizoram pawh hetiang hmun din nan hian Detailed Project Report leh a hmun tur (land site) rawn siam turin a sawm bawk a ni. Pu Rajiv-a chuan sawrkar laipuiin Mizoram sawrkarin Fish Marketing Center cheng vaibelchhe 50 senga din tura ngenna a lo thehluh chu an pawm thu a puang bawk a. Hemi bakah hian, Pu Rajiv-a chuan Sangha mawi khawi (Ornamental fisheries), Tui vawta sangha khawi (Coldwater fisheries) leh Tui tlinga sangha khawi (Inland fisheries) a hmalakna nasa tak kalpui theih a beisei thu a tarlang a. Sawrkar laipuiin hmarchhak bialah notified cluster 8 siamin cluster-based fish farming chawisan a duh thu te, Assam-a National Fisheries Development Board(NFDB) awmsa tihchangtlun leh he biala sangha khawitu zawng zawngte digital-a thlunzawm (digital onboarding) a duh thu te a sawi a ni. Minister chuan Hmarchhak biala Mission Amrit Sarovar atan cheng vaibelchhe 200-300 dah an inhuam thu a sawi a, fur zawh hnuah NDDB, NFDB leh Central hotu dangte chu hmarchhak state hrang hrang tlawh chhuak a, an mamawh leh theihnate hmuhpui turin a nawr dawn tih a tiam bawk a ni.

Ran vulh chungchangah Minister chuan hmarchhak state-te chuan Bawnghnute thar chhuahah zaa 37, Artui thar chhuahah zaa 40 leh Sa thar chhuahah zaa 15 in hmasawnna a awm thu a sawi a; amaherawhchu, Minister chuan hmarchhak state-te chu hmasawnna nasa zawk hmu zel turin tan la turin a fuih a ni. Minister chuan hmarchhaka khaw tinah Cooperative society-te chawisan a ngaihpawimawh thu leh Artificial Insemination (Ran kah rai), Infrastructure Development (Hmanrua leh hmunhma tihchangtlun), Technology din leh mihring thiamna (human capacity building) tihsan a ngaih thu a sawi bawk a. Rante zinga hri leng thin chungchang sawiin, Minister chuan sawrkar laipui chuan heng natna laka invenna vaccine-te siam chhuak turin theihtawp a chhuah reng thu a sawi a. Hemi bakah hian Guwahati-ah NDDB Regional Office din tum a nih thu a sawi bawk a ni.

Union Minister chuan Sangha khawi, Ran vulh leh Bawnghnute thar chhuah kawngah hmarchhak state-te hi mahni khamkhawp thar (self-sufficient) thei tura ruahmanna neih a nih thu sawiin, State minister-te leh official dangte chu sawrkar laipui nen thawk ho a, Schemes hrang hrang kalpui dan endik a, tihdan tha ber berte hmang a hmalakna hrang hrangte a hun taka tihpuitlin a nih theih nan hma la turin a sawm.

Minister of State Pu George Kurian pawhin thu sawiin, Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying Ministry chu hmarchhak mipuiten an eikhawp an hmuh theihna (Livelihood) atan te, Hmeichhiate dawmkanna (Women Empowerment) atan te leh Entrepreneurship atan tea thawktu a ni, a ti a. Hmarchhak state-te chu intodelh tura fuihin an state tharchhuahte chawilar lehzual turin a sawm a ni.

Department of Animal Husbandry & Dairying Secretary Pu Naresh Pal Gangwar pawhin thu sawiin, hmarchhak state-te intodelhna tura tanpui chu sawrkar laipui chuan a inhuam em em thu a sawi a. State tin te chu thahnemngai taka thawk tur leh sawrkar scheme hrang hrangte hlawhtlinna chu anmahniah a innghah thuizia hre turin a chah bawk.

Department of Fisheries Secretary Dr. Abhilaksh Likhi pawhin hmarchhak state-te chu thil thawnchhuahna hmunpui (export hubs) ni thei tura thuamchak zel tum a nih thu a sawi a. An mamawh apiangte inthlahrung lo a sawrkar laipui hnena thlen turin a sawm bawk a ni.

He hunah hian Hmarchhak state tin atangin Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying changtu Minister-ten an state theuha an dinhmun te, an mutanna te leh an hmachhawp te an sawi bawk a. Hmarchhak bial anga harsatna an tawhte an sawi bawk. Thalaite thuamchak (Youth Training) te, Cold chain storage neih te, Khawl thluaknei hman tangkai lehzual (Digital Deployment) te, Rotling thil siam (Infrastructure Development) te, a hun taka pawisa pekchhuah (Timely Fund Release) te chu pawimawh an tih thu an sawi a ni.

Vawiin hunah hian GAD Mizoram Principal Secretary leh Chief Secretary(i/c) ni bawk Pu Sudhir Kumar chuan kalkhawmte a lo lawm a. Fisheries Joint Secretary Pu Sagar Mehra chuan 'NER Vision 2030' tarlangin Dept. of Fisheries hnuaia Scheme-te leh Program hrang hrangte PowerPoint Presentation hmangin a sawifiah a. NDDB Chairman Pu Meenesh Shah-an North East Region-a Dairy Development lama hmachhawp awm te kalkhawmte a hrilhfiah a. Dept. of Animal Husbandry and Dairying Additional Secretary Pi Varsha Joshi-in Animal Husbandry huam chhunga hmalakna awm mek te leh hmachhawp awm te a sawi bawk a ni.

Vawiin hunah hian Multipurpose Dairy Cooperative Societies(MDCS) huam chin zauh tura baseline village level survey te, African Swine Fever chungchang film te, Vawk vulh dan inzirtirna lehkhabu te leh hmarchhak state-te tana scheme leh project hrang hrangte Union Minister chuan a tlangzarh a. NFDB Chief Executive Pu Bijay Kumar Behera chuan lawmthu sawiin he hun hi a khar a ni.

Attachment

title: SERCHHIP-AH DISTRICT LEVEL NARCO COORDINATION CENTRE  (NCORD) COMMITTEE THUKHAWM
content:
696/2026-2027

Dated Serchhip, the 25th May 2026 :Serchhip District Level Narco Coordination Centre (NCORD) Committee chu vawiin khan Serchhip DC Meeting Hall-ah an Chairman leh Deputy Commissioner ni bawk Pu Paul L. Khuma kaihhruaiin an ṭhukhawm.

Meeting-ah hian NCORD Member Secretary, Serchhip S Pu Gavit Gogna bakah Department hrang hrang aiawhte, Village Council leh tlawmngai pawl hruaitute an tel a ni.

Ṭhutkhawm kaihruaitu Pu Paul L. Khuma chuan ruihhlo dona kawngah sawrkar leh khawtlang hruaitute inkarah thawh hona ṭha tak awm ṭhin chu lawmawm a tih thu a sawi a. Department tinte chu theihtawp chhuah zel tura chahin, ‘hmalakna report felfai taka siam zel ni se’ a ti a. Dan bawhchhetute chungah hremna lekkawh a nih theih nan, thawh hona ṭha tak neih zel a pawimawh thu sawiin, dan ang loa chetna lakah fimkhur tlang tur leh dan ang taka chak taka hma la turin kalkhawmte chu a fuih a ni.

NCORD Member Secretary Pu Gavit Gogna pawhin Police Department-te chuan ruihhlo do nan theihtawp an chhuah zel tur thu sawiin, ruihhlo kaihhnawihah mi man leh rinhlelhte chhuizui zel a pawimawh thu a sawi a, Department dangte nena thawh hona ṭha tak nei tura an inhawn reng thu a sawi bawk.

Vawiin meeting-ah hian April 1, 2026 aṭanga Serchhip District chhunga hmalakna hrang hrangte thlir ho a ni a. Report-a tarlan a nih danin, Police department chuan hemi hun chhung hian drugs kaihhnawihah mi 4 manin, heroin gram 46.95, cheng 9,39,000 man hu leh zu chi hrang hrang cheng 1,66,800 man hu an man a. NGO-te nen ṭangrualin Serchhip, Thenzawl leh N. Vanlaiphai-ah te ruihhlo vaih hna (joint patrolling) nei bawkin, ruihhlo kaihhnawih thubuaiah mi 6 Court-ah an thawn bawk a, Anti-Drug Awareness vawi 4 an huaihawt bawk.

Excise & Narcotics Department lam pawhin mi 3 man bawkin, heroin gram 32.4 an man a, an mi man zinga pakhat chu hmeichhia niin, pakhat chu kum tling lo a ni a, Juvenile Justice Board-ah thlen a ni.

Vawiin meeting-ah hian department hrang hrangte hnen aṭangin ruihhlo dona kawnga an hmalakna leh hma chhawp hrang hrangte ngaihthlak a ni a; ruihhlo do inzirtirna (awareness) chak zawka kalpui dan tur te, ruihhlo ngaite chhanchhuah leh enkawl zui dan (rehabilitation) chungchang te, leh dan kenkawhna (enforcement) lam tihchak lehzual a nih theih dan turte sawiho a ni bawk.

Attachment

title: Pu Binod Kumar, Secretary, Election Commission of India chuan Champhai a tlawh
content:
695/2026-2027

Champhai dated 25th May, 2026: Vawiin khan Pu Binod Kumar, Secretary, Election Commission of India chuan Champhai a  tlawh a, Champhai District chhunga DEO, EROs, AEROs, BLO Supervisor leh BLO te chu Circuit House Conference Hall-ah Special Intensive Revision 2026 chungchangah review meeting a neihpui.

Pu Binod Kumar chuan Special Intensive Revision 2026 kalpui tan mekah hian BLO te'n mawhphurhna lian tak neiin anmahni ah an innghah tlat avangin theihtawp an chhuah a ngai tih a sawi a, Champhai hi Myanmar Border a awm a nih avangin SIR tihhlawhtlin hi a pawimawh lehzual tih sawiin SIR kalpui tura dan leh dun awmte hrechiang turin a chah a, awlsam zawk a an kalpui theihna tura ruahmanna felfai tak siam turin a chah a, an zawh hma leh an hahdam hma dawn avangin a hunlai chuan taima taka thawk tur leh enumeration form hi ni 5 chhunga semfel hman tum turin a chah a, duhsakna a hlan a ni.

He hun hi Pu Lalbiakfela, Addl. DC chuan kaihruaiin Pu Ngulzathuama, Election Officer chuan SIR puala hma an lo lak tawhna te report a pek bakah Champhai District-a Assembly Constituency awm te leh heng Constituency-a polling station awm zat leh Voter awm dan te a tarlang bawk.

Pu Binod Kumar hian vawiin vek khan Zokhawthar Polling Station a tlawh nghal a, VC leh YMA hruaitute'n an lo hmuak a inkawmhona an nei nghal a,  Pu Ngulzathuam EO, Pu Lalchhuanawma Ralte SDC leh I&PR Officials ten an tawiawm a ni.

Attachment

title: Special Intensive Revision, 2026 atan Booth Level Officer Supervisors leh Booth Level Officers te tan vawiin khan Circuit House Conference Hall ah training buatsaih a ni.
content:
694/2026-2027

He hun hi Pu Ngulzathuama, Election Officer chuan kaihruaiin Booth level standing committee din ngei ngei tur a nih thu leh chu committee ah chuan khawtlang hruaitu te an tel a ngaih thu sawiin, thlakhat chhung chauh hun an neih avangin a hautak em em dawn a, committee anga hma an lak loh chuan mipui tam tak tan harsatna an thlen thei dawn tih a sawi.

Pu James Lalnithanga, ERO, 23 Champhai North chuan Booth Level Supervisor leh Booth Level Officer kalkhawmte chu an mawhphurhna a san dan leh mipui ten vote an neih zelna tura mawhphurhna zawng zawng an koah a awm avangin theihtawp chhuaha an mawhphurhna hlenchhuak ngei turin kalkhawmte a fuih ve bawk.

Dr. Peter Malsawmtluanga, ERO, 25 East Tuipui A/C leh District Level Master  Trainer leh Dr Vanlalnupuii AERO 23 Champhai North A/C ni bawk te chuan kalkhawmte an zirtir a, SIR kalpui dan tur te, BLO App hman dan turte chipchiar taka ngaihthlak a ni a, SIR tih hunchhung hian Enumeration form te chu online hmanga upload a ngaihdan turte, hun tiam chhunga upload hman loh an nei a nih chuan an upload loh chin zawng chu Draft roll atanga paih tur an nih thu leh a hranpaa form lachhuak a, dil that leh a ngai dawn a, document tam tak form hian a phut tawh avangin mipuite tan harsatna tam tak a siam dawn avangin hun tiam chhunga an tih turte an hlenchhuah ngei ngei a ngaihdan te, tun tum SIR hunchhung hi chuan document thil tel ngai lovin form hmangin voter's ID nei tawhte chu ziah luh mai theih an nih avangin tan an lak nasa a tul thu te a tarlang bawk.

Special Intensive Revision 2026 ah hian voter's ID nei tawh zawng zawngte pawhin enumeration form an fillup that leh vek a ngai dawn a, BLO te'n in tinah kalin enumeration form an semchhuak ang a, BLO te vek hian an khawnkhawm leh dawn a, an bial chhunga form dahkhah dan thiam lote an pui nghal ang, huntiam chhunga Form dahkhat lo emaw thehlut lo an awm a nih chuan Electoral Draft Roll atanga paih an ni dawn a ni.

Attachment

title: KOLASIB DISTRICT NCORD THUTKHAWMNA NEIH A NI
content:
693/2026-2027

Kolasib, 25th May, 2026:Vawiin khan DC Conference Hall, Kolasib-ah Kolasib Deputy Commissioner Pu H. Dolianbuaia hovin District Narco Coordination Centre (NCORD) thutkhawmna neih a ni.

Thutkhawm kaihruaitu DC chuan kalkhawmte chu ruihhlo buaipuitu tura sawrkar ruat kan ni tih inhriat nawn a pawimawh thu a sawi a. Ruihhlo do kawnga hma la mek tute hnathawh chu fakawm a tih thu sawiin, kohhran lam te pawhin hetiang lama tan an la chho zel chu lawmawm takzet a ni a ti a.

Hemi chungchanga hmalak zelnaah hian mithiam zawk leh tawnhriat  nei tha te chu resource person atana hman tangkai a that thu a sawi bawk.

NCORD Member Secretary, Kolasib Superintendent of Police (SP) Pu Lalrinpuia Varte chuan thutkhawm chhan sawifiahna hun hmangin ruihhlo man a harsat thu a sawi a. Ruihhlo lian tham (commercial quantity) an man thin te hi a ngawlveite atanga man ni lovin, sumdawnna atana hmanga nghet taka kalpuitu, ruihhlo ti ve ngai miah lote hnen atanga man an nih tlangpui thu a sawi a. Hemi a nih avang hian intelligence collection a pawimawh hle a, chumi atan chuan khawtlangin tan kan rual a ngai a ni, a ti. SP thusawi zawh hian aiawh lo kal hrang hrangte hnen atangin ruihhlo dona kawnga an hmalakna report ngaihthlak a ni.

Pu R. Lalnunpuia, SDPO Kolasib chuan report pein, kumin May ni 10 khan North Chhimluang khaw bulah Bolero Pick-Up (Registration No. AS11GC 5383) atangin zu (IMFL) litre 1560, a vaiin case 164 man a nih thu a tarlang a. Heng zu man te hi a rate tarlan danin (printed price) cheng 7,90,800 man hu a ni. He thil thleng chungchangah hian Vairengte Police Station-ah thubuai ziah luh a ni a, zu neitute leh a tawlhtute hi chhui zui mek an ni a ti.

Excise & Narcotics Department atanga report tarlan danin, kumin April ni 1 atanga May ni 23 inkar khan zu chi hrang hrang leh a kaihhnawih cheng 48,31,615 man hu leh Heroin cheng 38,16,096 man hu an man a. He hun chhung hian Mizoram Liquor Prohibition (MLP) Act hnuaiah thubuai (case) 541 ziah luh a ni a, NDPS Act hnuaiah case 1 ziah luh a ni bawk. Sub-Headquarters YMA, Kolasib pawhin report pein, ruihhlo dona kawng sawiho tur hian June ni 23 khian kohhran hrang hrang hruaitute nena hun hman an tum thu an tarlang a. Hemi bakah hian Golgotha Camping Centre leh TNT Kolasib aiawhte pawhin ruihhlo ngaite chhanchhuahna kawnga an hmalak dan tlangpui te an sawi bawk.Heng bakah hian Kalkhawmte chuan ruihhlo dona kawnga thawhhona tha zawk leh hmalak dan tur tha zawk an neih theih dan tur te an sawiho nghal a ni.

Vawiin thutkhawmah hian Police leh Excise department hotute bakah CMO Kolasib, Sub-Headquarters YMA aiawhte, ruihhlo ngaite enkawlna (Rehabilitation Home) hrang hrang aiawhte leh District Drug Inspector te an tel a ni.

Attachment

title: COOPERATION MINISTER PU PC VANLALRUATA’N HARDWARE DISTRIBUTION PROGRAMME HMANPUI
content:
692/2026-2027

Dated Aizawl, the 25th May 2026:Vawiin khan Cooperation Minister Pu P.C.Vanlalruata chuan Computerisation of Primary Agricultural Credit Cooperative Societies (PACS) Phase-II hnuaia Centrally Sponsored Project kalpui mek Hardware Distribution Programme chu RCS Conference Hall, Babutlang-a neihah khuallian niin a hmanpui. He hunah hian Cooperation Department Commissioner & Secretary Pu Udit Prakash Rai chu khual zahawm a ni.

Minister chuan Cooperative Society-te tana hardware semna hun a hmanpui thei chu lawmawm a tih thu a sawi a. Cooperative Society-te pawimawhzia sawiin, ram hmasawnna tur atana pawimawh ber zinga mi an ni, a ti a. Khawvel hmasawn zelin a ken tel digitaization lama hma an sawnna tur kailawn pawimawh tak a nih avangin he an hardware dawnte pawh tangkai taka hmang turin a chah a. Mizoramin hmasawnna rahbi thar a chuankai theihna atana entawntlak (model) ni tura ke pen turin a fuih a ni.

Pu P.C. Vanlalruata chuan Society-te hmasawn zelna atan Chief Minister pawmpuina angin sawrkar laipui Ministry of Cooperation hnenah Mizoram Cooperative Policy an duan chu thehluh tawh a nih thu a sawi a. Cooperative Society-te chuan sawrkar atangin a tlem berah cheng nuai 1 tal puihna an dawn theihna tur scheme a, tiin, he dilna hi a hlawhtling a nih chuan Mizorama Cooperative Society-te tan a tangkai hle dawn a ni, a ti.

Khual zahawm Cooperation Department Commissioner & Secretary Pu Udit Prakash Rai chuan Mizorama Cooperative Society ten hardware an dawng thei chu hmasawnna nasa tak a ni a, PACS-te chu Mizoram hmasawnna atana laimu an ni, a ti. Mizoramah hian PACS 270 bawr vel an awm mek thu leh heng PACS 270 te zawng zawng hian computer an la neih vek theih nana thawh a inhuam thu a sawi a. Cooperative Society chu Mizoram than zelna tura fawng pawimawh tak vuantu a nih thu a sawi bawk a ni.

Computerisation of PACS Project hi Ministry of Cooperation hnuaia Centrally Sponsored Project kalpui mek niin Phase-II ah hian PACS 24 computerised tura thlanchhuah an ni a, field verification pawh tih zawh tawh a ni. Hardware semte hi sawrkar thuchhuah ding lai angin GeM Portal kaltlang veka supplier-te hnen atanga lei an ni a. Saaj Venture, Dimapur-te chu supplier niin vawiina hardware an sem hi item 10 - Desktop HP Pro Tower 280 G9, inverter, dot matrix passbook printer, HP laser printer, thermal printer, fingerprint scanner, barcode scanner, POS machine, webcam leh tablet te a ni a. Heng PACS computerized te hian ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning) software kaltlangin hna an thawk tawh dawn a ni.

Vawiin programme hi Cooperative Societies Registrar Pu John LT Sanga chuan Chairman niin a kaihruai a, Computerization of PACS Nodal Officer Pi L.Lalzidingi chuan report pein, PACS aiawha thusawi tawi Pu Lalnunsanga (Melriat MPACS) leh Pu Lalngaihawma (Chhuanthar MPACS) atangin ngaihthlak a ni a, Additional Registrar Pu A.N.Barbhuiya chuan lawmthu sawiin he programme hi a khar a ni.

title: KHAWZAWLAH ‘JAN BHAGIDARI – SABSE DOOR, SABSE PEHLE’ CAMPAIGN KHARNA NEIH A NI
content:
691/2026-2027

Dated Khawzawl, the 25th May, 2026 :Vawiin khan Khawzawl Bawrhsap Pu K. Vanlalthiana kaihhruaiin DC Conference Hall-ah PM JANMAN leh DAJGUA hnuaia ‘Jan Bhagidari – Sabse Door, Sabse Pehle’ Campaign kharna inkhawm neih a ni.

Bawrhsap Pu K. Vanlalthiana chuan he campaign a hlawhtlin theih nana thawktu line department-te chungah lawmthu a sawi a. Mipui tam zawkin sawrkar scheme hrang hrang an hmelhriata hamthatna an dawn ve theih nan awareness nei zel a pawimawh thu a sawi.

Vawiin inkhawmah hian department hrang hrangten he campaign hnuaia an hmalak dan report an pe a. DAO hmalaknain RKVY-PDMC hnuaiah Rabung-ah tui pipe semchhuahna te, Sialhawk leh Chawngtlai-ah PM-KISAN awareness leh Soil Health Card sem chhuahna hunt e neih a ni a. PHED Khawzawl hmalaknain Khualen khua-ah engtiklai pawha tui herh haw a nih theih nan tui meter vuah a ni a. DFDO hmalaknain tui sawngbawl dan, luidung nungcha an inthlahpun laia humhalh dan tur leh sangha chi leh chaw subsidised rate-a leih theih a nih thu hriattirna hunt e neih a ni a. DCSO hmalaknain Anganwadi hrang hrangahte Wheat Based Nutrition Program kalpui a ni a, mipuite ration card chungchanga harsatna an neihte chinfel sak an ni bawk. Heng bakah hian CMO, DEO, P&E leh department dangten hmalakna tha tak tak an nei a ni.

He hunah hian YMA Sub-Headquarters, MUP leh MHIP Sub-Headquarters aiawhte an tel a. Sawrkar official-te nen mipuite tana tha lehzuala hmalak dan turte an sawiho. SDC Pu R. Lalrinhlua chuan Khawzawl district chu he campaign atan Mizoram chhunga hma la tha ber pawh a nih thu a tarlang a, lawmthu sawiin hun a khar a ni.

title: SIR 2026 atan BLO training buatsaih
content:
690/2026-2027

Aizawl the 25th May, 2026:Special Intensive Revision (SIR) 2026 tluang taka kalpui a nih theih nan ni thum chhung Booth Level Officers (BLO), Assistant/Additional Booth Level Officers (ABLO) leh BLO Supervisor te tana training buatsaih a ni.

May ni 25, 26 leh 28 chhung hian Aizawl khawpui chhungah I&PR Auditorium, Pisapui Hall, School Education Conference Hall leh BDO Conference Hall, Darlawn-ahte training neih a ni ang a. Training-ah hian Aizawl District chhunga Assembly Constituency hrang hrangte an tel dawn a ni.

May 25 chawhmaah Aizawl North-I, Aizawl East-I leh Aizawl West-I Assembly Constituency aiawhte an training a, chawhnu session-ah Aizawl North-II, Aizawl East-II leh Aizawl West-II Constituency aiawhten training an nei thung a ni.

May 26 chawhmaah Aizawl South-I leh Aizawl West-III Constituency aiawhten training an nei ang a, chawhnu session-ah Aizawl South-II leh Aizawl South-III Constituency aiawhte training an nei leh ang. Tuivawl (ST) Assembly Constituency atan pawh BDO Conference Hall, Darlawn-ah training a hranpaa neih a ni bawk ang.

May 28-ah hian Aizawl North-III (ST) Assembly Constituency huam chhung BLO te tan Pisapui Hall, DC Office, Aizawl-ah training session hnuhnung neih a ni ang.

Training hi District Level Master Trainers (DLMT) — Dr. Lalchamliani, BDO Tlangnuam; Pi Lalruatpuii Hnamte, DCSO Aizawl East; Pi Christina Lalmuanzuali, Under Secretary, Finance Department; leh Pu Hmingmawizuala, Assistant Commissioner ten an kaihruai ang.

Special Intensive Revision (SIR) 2026-ah hian BLO te chu electoral roll thar dik leh felfai buatsaih kawngah mawhphurhna sang tak an nei dawn a. May ni 30 atanga fehchhuak tanin, BLO te hian Enumeration Form sem, voter-te hnen atanga form lakkhawm, in tin tlawh chhuah leh voter te detail tihdik hna an thawk ang a. Electoral roll tharah vote nei thei zawng zawng, hmaih nei loa an tel vek theih nan mawhphurhna sang tak an nei dawn a ni.

title: KHAWZAWL DAMC MEETING NEI
content:
689/2026-2027

Dated Khawzawl, the 25thMay, 2026 :Vawiin khan Khawzawl Bawrhsap Pu K. Vanlalthiana, District Agriculture Marketing Committee (DAMC) Chairman ni bawk kaihhruaiin DC Conference Hall-ah DAMC meeting neih a ni.

Vawiin thutkhawmah hian Secondary Collection Centre tin Supervisor-ten sawhthing an hralh tawh zat report pe-in, SCC tam zawkah hralh tur a awm tawh loh thu an sawi a. DAMC Chairman Pu K. Vanlalthiana chuan Supervisor-te thawhrimna chu fakawm a tih thu a sawi a. Loneitu sawhthing thar tam ber leh Supervisor thawk tha ber lawmman buatsaih tura rel a ni.

He hunah hian Addl DC Pu K. Zorammuana, SDO Pu Chandrakant Bagoria, Project Director Pu K. Lalhmuakliana, Khawzawl District Agriculture Officer leh DAMC Member-Secretary ni bawk Pi Lallawmuali leh SCC Supervisor-te an tel a ni.

title: CHIEF MINISTER CHAIRS MEETING ON GINGER USP PROJECT
content:
106/2026-2027

Aizawl, 25 May 2026:Chief Minister Pu Lalduhoma today chaired a meeting at the CM Conference Hall to discuss the implementation of the Ginger USP Project. The meeting was attended by Pu PC Vanlalruata, Minister for Agriculture; Pu F. Rodingliana, Minister for Industries; Prof. Lalnilawma, Minister for Rural Development; Advisers to the Chief Minister Pu TBC Lalvenchhunga and Dr. KC Lalmalsawmzauva; along with senior officials.

The Ginger USP Project will be implemented through convergence among around five departments. Under the project, the main ginger processing centre will be established at Mualkawi in Champhai district. In addition, three smaller processing units, or “Spoke Hubs,” will be set up at Tualcheng, Zotlang, and Vaphai to facilitate preliminary processing before the produce reaches the main centre.

At the Spoke Hubs, ginger will undergo cleaning, grading, slicing, and drying. Activities that cannot be carried out at these centres, including oil extraction, branding, and other advanced processing, will be undertaken at the main processing facility at Mualkawi.

The project aims to complete installation of the machinery within this year. The processing units are planned to be operated not directly by the government but by private investors, entrepreneurs, and societies.

During today’s meeting, detailed discussions were held regarding timelines, coordination mechanisms, and all necessary steps for successful implementation of the project.

As announced earlier, the Ministry of Development of North Eastern Region (DoNER) has selected ginger as Mizoram Unique Selling Proposition (USP). Based on this recognition, the “Project Mizoram Ginger Sustainable Cultivation & Value Chain Development” has been formulated with an estimated outlay of Rs. 189.78 crore. Out of this, the North Eastern Council (NEC) has already sanctioned Rs. 30.13 crore for establishment of the processing units.

The Sustainable Cultivation component of the project will be implemented in collaboration with the Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare, Ministry of Rural Development, Ministry of Food Processing Industries (MoFPI), NABARD, ICAR, and IFAD.

The Ginger USP Project now being taken up marks the first phase of implementation, with the possibility of extending similar initiatives to other districts in the future.

Attachment

title: CHIEF MINISTER HOVIN GINGER USP PROJECT CHUNGCHANG SAWIHO
content:
688/2026-2027

Aizawl the 25th May 2026:Vawiin khan Chief Minister Pu Lalduhoma hovin CM Conference Hall-ah Ginger USP Project kalpui dan tur sawihona neih a ni a. Pu PC Vanlalruata, Agriculture Minister, Pu F. Rodingliana, Industry Minister, Prof Lalnilawma, RD Minister, Pu TBC Lalvenchhunga leh Dr. KC Lalmalsawmzauva, Adviser to CM te leh official pawimawhte an tel.

Ginger USP Project hi department 5 velin thawkhoin (Convergence) an kalpui dawn a, Champhai Mualkawiah Sawhthing sawngbawlna pui ber dah a ni ang a, a process-na pui ber a thlen hmaa lo bunlawkna tur a process-na tenau pathum (spoke hub) Tualcheng, Zotlang leh Vaphaiah te dah a ni ang. Spoke Hub-ah hian sawhthingte chu tihfai a, hralh nghal chi leh hralh nghal mai chi loh thliar (grading) a ni ang a, a channa (slice) a awm bawk ang a, a tihrona a awm bawk ang. Chutianga tih theih loh chin, a oil siamnate, a branding te leh a dangte chu a hmunpui ber Mualkawiah thawh a ni ang.

Kumin chhungin a khawl bun fel hman tum a ni a. A khawlte hi sorkar enkawl lo va mimal investor/ entreprenuers / socuety te enkawltir tura duan a ni.

Vawiin meeting-ah hian he project kalpui dan tur indawt leh huntiam neia thawh ngai chi zawng zawngte chu chipchiar taka sawi a ni.

Kan lo tarlan tawh angin Ministry of DoNER chuan Mizoram sawhthing chu Unique Selling Proposition a pe a, chumi behchhan Project Mizoram Ginger Sustainable Cultivation & Value Chain Development, Rs.189.78 Crore senna tur siam niin, he project chhunga hmalakna pakhat Processing Unit dinna tur hi DoNER chuan NEC-ah Rs. 30.13 Crore a sanction tawh a. Sustainable Cultivation hi Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare te, Ministry of Rural Development te, Ministry of Food Processing Industry te, NABARD, ICAR leh IFAD te nena thawkhoa kalpui tur a ni.

Ginger USP Project, tuna kalpui tur hi a thawhkhatna a ni a. District dangah pawh kalpui zui zel theih beisei a ni.

title: JAN BHAGIDAARI IEC CAMPAIGN “SABSE DOOR, SABSE PEHLE” CHU SIAHA DISTRICT AH HLAWHTLING TAKIN ZAWH FEL A NI
content:
687/2026-2027

Dateh Siaha 25thMay 2026:PM JANMAN leh DA-JGUA hnuaia buatsaih Jan Bhagidaari IEC Campaign “Sabse Door, Sabse Pehle” chu Siaha District ah May Ni 18 atanga Ni 25 2026 thleng District Administration, Siaha enkawlna hnuaiah hlawhtling taka kalpui a ni a. He campaign hi Ministry of Tribal Affairs, Government of India thupek anga kalpui a ni.

Campaign thil tum ber chu hmun kilkhawr ber thlenga chengte chu sawrkar atanga hamthatna scheme hrang hrang hriattir a, an chanvo dik tak an chan theih nana tanpui a ni. Campaign hi Siaha RD Block leh Tipa RD Block huamchhunga khaw 27 ah kalpui a ni a, IEC campaign hmun 12 ah buatsaih niin ASK Centre 27 ruahman a ni. IEC Campaign, Jan Sunwai programme, Benefit Saturation & Health Camp, leh Transect Walk te DAJGUA gap village-ah neih a ni bawk. He campaignah hian mipui 2,830 vel an tel a ni.

VC, SHG, ASHA Worker, Anganwadi Worker, NGO, kohhran hruaitute, thalai pawl leh khawtlang mipuite an tel tha hle a. hriattur pawimawh hrang hrang PM JANMAN , Ayushman Bharat, Aadhaar service, ration card  leh sawrkar scheme dangte chu flyer, poster leh tawngkaa hrilhfiahin hrilhhriat an ni. Jan Sunwai  hun buatsaih chuan official-te leh mipuite inpawhna tur kawng tha tak siamin hriselna kawng, kawngpui, tui thianghlim, zirna, eizawnna, leh hmasawnna ruhrel chungchangah mipuiten an mamawh leh duhthusam an thlen thei a ni. Transect Walk chu official-te, khawtlang hruaitute leh mipuite tangrualin neih a ni. ASK hmun hrang hrangah lungawilohna thlenna tur (help desk)  leh facilitation counter din a ni bawk a. Mipuite harsatna chu department kaihhnawihah rang taka thlen theiha buatsaih a ni.

Closing-cum-Debriefing Programme chu May Ni 25 2026 khan DC Conference Room, Siaha ah neih a ni a. Deputy Commissioner, Siaha in a kaihruai a, line department hotute leh chanchinbumite an tel. Campaign kalpui dan bakah an harsatna tawh te  leh hmun kilkhawr ber thlenga sawrkar inrelbawlna tihchak zel dan tur ngaihtuahho a ni. District Administration chuan department zawng zawng, frontline worker-te, pawl hrang hrang, khawtlang hruaitute, volunteer-te leh mipuite thawhpuina tha avanga campaign hlawhtling taka a kal thei chu lawmthu a sawi a ni.

Attachment

title: Jan Bhagidaari IEC Campaign “Sabse Door, Sabse Pehle” Successfully concluded in Siaha District
content:
105/2026-2027

Siaha the 25th May 2026:The Jan Bhagidaari IEC Campaign titled “Sabse Door, Sabse Pehle” under PM JANMAN & DA-JGUA was successfully implemented in Siaha District from 18th to 25th May 2026 under the supervision of the District Administration, Siaha, in accordance with the directives of the Ministry of Tribal Affairs, Government of India.

The campaign aimed at strengthening awareness generation, improving outreach of government welfare schemes, and ensuring last-mile delivery of services to tribal communities residing in remote villages and habitations across the district.

The campaign covered Siaha RD Block and Tipa RD Block, reaching 27 villages and conducting activities at 12 IEC campaign locations. A total of 27 ASK Centres were identified for outreach and facilitation activities. During the campaign period, various activities including IEC Campaigns, Jan Sunwai programmes, Benefit Saturation & Health Camps, and Transect Walks were conducted in DAJGUA gap villages. Approximately 2,830 people participated in the awareness programmes, public interaction sessions, health camps, and outreach activities organized under the campaign.

The campaign witnessed active participation from Village Councils, SHGs, ASHA Workers, Anganwadi Workers, NGOs, church leaders, youth groups, and local residents. Awareness regarding PM JANMAN, Ayushman Bharat, Aadhaar services, ration card facilities, and other government welfare schemes was disseminated through field-level interactions, awareness meetings, banners, posters, and local communication methods.

Jan Sunwai programmes provided an effective platform for direct interaction between officials and beneficiaries, enabling community members to share concerns and developmental aspirations related to healthcare, road connectivity, drinking water, education, livelihood opportunities, and rural infrastructure.Transect Walks jointly conducted by officials, village leaders, and community members facilitated on-site assessment of village conditions and infrastructure gaps while strengthening grassroots-level engagement. Dedicated grievance help desks and facilitation counters were also established at various ASK locations to assist beneficiaries and ensure timely forwarding of grievances to concerned departments.

A Closing-cum-Debriefing Programme of the campaign was held on 25th May 2026 at the DC Conference Room, Siaha, chaired by the Deputy Commissioner, Siaha. Heads of Offices of line departments attended the programme along with members of the press. The meeting reviewed the overall implementation of the campaign, field-level achievements, challenges encountered, and future strategies for strengthening outreach initiatives in remote tribal areas.

The District Administration expressed appreciation to all departments, frontline workers, local institutions, village authorities, volunteers, and community members for their active cooperation and successful implementation of the campaign.

The campaign significantly strengthened participatory governance, departmental convergence, and direct engagement between the administration and tribal communities, thereby promoting inclusive development and improved accessibility to public welfare services in remote areas of Siaha District.

Attachment

title: TUIALHTHEI INRENCHEM CHUNGCHANGA LAWNGTLAI BAWRHSAP THUCHHUAH
content:
686/2026-2027

Lawngtlai, 23rd May 2026 :Tuialhthei inrenchem chungchangah a hnuaia mi ang hian Lawngtlai Bawrhsap chuan thuchhuah a siam.

ORDER

No.J.11013/1/2026- DC(LTI): Tunlaiin West Asia ram boruak enin leh Mizoram Sawrkar thuchhuah No. C.1315/2/2025 - ESTT- GAD Dt.19.05.26 angin tuialhthei inremchen ngaiin kan District ah pawh hmalak na heti zawnga kalpui a lo ngai ta ani.

Chuvangin, Kei Donny Lalruatsanga, MCS, District Magistrate hian Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita, 2023 (BNSS), Section 163 in thuneihna min pek angin he khapna thupek hi ka chhuah e.

1. Lawngtlai District chhunga Filling Station tin ten, Sawrkar in man (rate) a bithliah aia sang (Black Marketing) in Tuialhthei (Petrol leh Diesel) an hralh tur a ni lo.

2. Lawngtlai District chhunga Filling Station tin ten mipui nawlpui, license kher ngailo tan nikhatah Petrol litres 30 leh Diesel litres 1000(a burin) an pe chhuak tur a ni.

3. Tuialhthei (Petrol leh Diesel) te hi kan thenawm ram Myanmar leh ram dangah sumdawnna kaihhnawih thilah hralh leh pek chhawn khap tlat a ni. Hei hian a bik taka permit pek a huam lovang.

4. Lawngtlai District chhunga dan lova tuialhthei lei leh phur khap tlat a ni.

He thupek zawm lo chu Section. 223 of Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS) hmangin hrem theih an ni.

He thupek hian Lawngtlai District pum a huam anga, thu leh awm hma chu tihchhuah anih atang thlahnih(2) chhung a huam ang.

He thupek hi vawiin Dt.22.05.2026 hian District Magistrate Seal- a nemnghetin hming ka ziah hnan e.

Sd/-

DONNY LALRUATSANGA

District Magistrate

Lawngtlai District, Lawngtlai
